Class AsyncServer

java.lang.Object
com.server.network.AsyncServer
public class AsyncServer extends Object
Non-blocking TCP server using Java NIO (New I/O). This server accepts client connections and processes requests using asynchronous I/O operations. Unlike traditional blocking servers, this can handle many concurrent connections without creating a thread for each client. Key features: - Uses AsynchronousServerSocketChannel for non-blocking accept - Uses AsynchronousSocketChannel for non-blocking read/write - Maintains a set of all connected clients - Provides server statistics via getStats() Communication flow: 1. Server starts and listens on specified port 2. Client connects via AsynchronousSocketChannel 3. Server accepts connection and starts reading 4. Server reads request, processes it, sends response 5. Server waits for next request or client disconnect

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AsyncServer(int port)
    Creates a new server on the specified port.

  • Constructor Details

    • AsyncServer

      public AsyncServer(int port)
      Creates a new server on the specified port.
      Parameters:
      port - The port number to listen on (e.g., 8080)

  • Method Details

    • start

      public void start() throws IOException
      Starts the server and begins accepting clients. This is a non-blocking operation - the method returns immediately while the server continues running in background.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the server cannot bind to the port

    • getStats

      public ServerStats getStats()
      Gets current server statistics. This is called when a client sends a HEALTH request.
      Returns:
      ServerStats containing current server state

    • stop

      public void stop() throws IOException
      Stops the server and disconnects all clients.
      Throws:
      IOException - If there's an error closing the server

    • isRunning

      public boolean isRunning()
      Checks if the server is currently running.
      Returns:
      true if server is running, false otherwise

    • getPort

      public int getPort()
      Gets the port number the server is listening on.
      Returns:
      The port number
